ocr.text: The following data table presents a comprehensive analysis of genetic variant rs11722228, specifically focusing on its impact
within the SLC2A9 gene. This analysis includes key metrics such as the Minor Allele Frequency (MAF) and Effect Size
(s.e.m.), which are critical for understanding the variant's potential influence on phenotypic traits. The MAF indicates the
frequency of the minor allele within the studied population, while the Effect Size quantifies the magnitude of the variant's
impact, adjusted for standard error of measurement. Additionally, the table illustrates various methodological approaches
employed in the analysis, including Filter Method, Wrapper Methods, and Hybrid Methods, which provide insight into the
robustness of the findings.
SNP  Chr  bp  Genes  Locus  Minor/major allele  GWAS(n = 3,451)  Validation(n = 8,830)  Combined(n = 12,281)
MAF  Effect size (s.e.m.)  P- valuea  MAF  Effect size (s.e.m.)  P-valuea  P- valuea
Filter Method  Wrapper Methods  Hybrid Methods
Criterion  Fisher's Ratio  RFE-LNW-GD  RFE-SVM  RFE-LSSVM  RFE-RR  RFE-FLDA  RFE-LNW1  RFE-LNW2  RFE-FSVs-7DK
Number of genes selected  6  17  4  33  7  2  2  70  12
Architecture Options  Execution Time  Estimated Required Resources
(# of Clocks)  DSPs  Block RAMs  LUTs

Notably, the P-values associated with these analyses serve as indicators of statistical significance, allowing researchers to assess
the likelihood that the observed associations are due to chance. By integrating these diverse metrics, the table offers a nuanced
view of the genetic landscape surrounding rs11722228, facilitating further exploration into its biological implications and
potential therapeutic targets.
